name: Akershus (Norway, 1987–2019)

colors.symbolism: the field of the county arms

description: A blue field charged with a stylised silver representation of the towers and walls of Akershus Fortress rising from the bottom edge.
history: Granted to Akershus county on 22 May 1987, designed by Hallvard Tratteberg. The county was abolished on 1 January 2020 when it was merged into the new Viken county; Akershus was re-established on 1 January 2024 following Viken's dissolution.

features.symbolism: heraldic banner of the county arms: a silver (argent) representation of Akershus Fortress on a blue field, the fortress that gave the county its name
the medieval fortress of Akershus in Oslo, after which the county is named
